concerns.text: Task T029 explicitly states validation against independent ChIP-seq is 'impossible' and plans to generate a 'validation_gap_report' instead. This directly violates Spec FR-003 and SC-003, which mandate validation against an independent held-out ChIP-seq dataset to identify non-canonical features. The task deliverable (a report of failure) falls short of the required deliverable (validated features).

Task T014 mandates excluding cell types with missing ATAC-seq data. Spec FR-001 requires ingesting data for ≥5 cell types. If Phase 2 finds 5 cell types but only 3 have full multi-modal data, T014 ensures the final dataset has <5 cell types, violating the hard constraint of FR-001. The task does not include a mechanism to search for additional cell types to meet the minimum threshold.

Task T006 proposes revising the scope to a 'sequence-only model' if data is missing. The Plan.md explicitly states that a sequence-only model 'would fundamentally change the research question' and violates Constitution Principle VI (Biological Validity). This task proposes a solution that the plan and constitution explicitly reject as invalid, creating a semantic contradiction where the task plan violates the project's core constraints.

Task T039 (cleanup) and T040 (integration test) do not include an explicit step to profile and enforce the 6-hour runtime constraint mandated by Spec SC-004. Without a dedicated task to measure and enforce this limit during training (T021) and permutation (T031), the success criterion (SC-004) is not preserved in the execution plan.

Phase 2 (T004-T007) focuses on searching and validating sources but lacks a concrete task to retrieve, checksum, and verify the integrity of the actual data files before proceeding. Spec Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) and Principle II (Verified Accuracy) require checksumming and verification of external datasets. The current tasks stop at 'validation of URL accessibility' without ensuring the data itself is verified and checksummed as required.
